name: ssg-ai-backbone
description: Sopra Steria Group AI SDLC Foundation — cross-provider collection of agents, skills, workflows, prompts, and foundational knowledge for specification-driven delivery, quality validation, security governance, brand compliance, and full-lifecycle SDLC support. Designed for reuse as an MCP server across projects with dynamic workflows covering the entire software development lifecycle.

plugins.name: ssg-ai-backbone
plugins.description: Complete AI SDLC Foundation — all agents, skills, workflows, prompts, instructions and foundational knowledge for specification-driven delivery, quality validation, security governance, and full-lifecycle SDLC support.

plugins.name: analysis-agent
plugins.description: Diagnose production incidents by analyzing logs, traces, and identifying root causes.

plugins.name: architecture-governance
plugins.description: Review specifications and plans against architecture principles and guardrails.

plugins.name: bmad-orchestrator
plugins.description: Drive BMAD feedback loop with quality scoring and adaptive decision-making.

plugins.name: branding
plugins.description: Audit, refactor, and generate brand-compliant applications, documents, and presentations. Default brand: Sopra Steria.

plugins.name: bug-fixer
plugins.description: Drive structured bug diagnosis and resolution with regression testing.

plugins.name: hub-orchestrator
plugins.description: Central triage agent — discovers available workflows and agents, classifies user intent, and dispatches execution.

plugins.name: implementer
plugins.description: Execute implementation tasks by reading task breakdowns and producing code.

plugins.name: modernization-agent
plugins.description: Guide modernization initiatives through baseline assessment and migration planning.

plugins.name: modernization-orchestrator
plugins.description: Coordinate modernization sub-agents for assessment, planning, and validation.

plugins.name: pr-validator
plugins.description: Run deterministic merge request validation checks including frontmatter, naming conventions, YAML lint, and test gap detection.

plugins.name: quality-validator
plugins.description: Execute quality and security validation using external tool adapters.

plugins.name: refactor-assessor
plugins.description: Comprehensive codebase assessment producing structured as-is documentation for refactoring.

plugins.name: refactor-implementer
plugins.description: Execute individual migration tasks from the approved plan using the appropriate skill for each task.

plugins.name: refactor-orchestrator
plugins.description: Foundation orchestrator that gathers decisions, creates ADRs, plans, and delegates refactoring to sub-agents.

plugins.name: refactor-parity-checker
plugins.description: Verify refactored application matches original behavior with systematic comparison.

plugins.name: refactor-planner
plugins.description: Analyse ADRs and as-is assessment to produce a detailed, phased migration plan with dependency ordering.

plugins.name: repository-analyzer
plugins.description: Generate high-level architectural and functional overview from repository analysis.

plugins.name: reverse-backlog
plugins.description: Extract business capabilities and generate structured product backlog from codebase.

plugins.name: reverse-user-story
plugins.description: Generate detailed user stories with acceptance criteria from existing codebase.

plugins.name: sdlc-ba-analyst
plugins.description: Transform business needs into complete functional specification through structured pipeline.

plugins.name: sdlc-ba-reviewer
plugins.description: Independent reviewer for BA deliverables — validates quality, coherence, and traceability with conflict escalation to human.

plugins.name: sdlc-coordinator
plugins.description: Orchestrate the full SDLC harness with DAG resolution and wave scheduling.

plugins.name: sdlc-steer-manager
plugins.description: Provide project steering, sprint tracking, and release governance decisions.

plugins.name: sdlc-steer-reviewer
plugins.description: Independent reviewer for Steer deliverables — validates cross-domain accuracy of governance documents and Go/No-Go decisions with conflict escalation to human.

plugins.name: sdlc-tech-architect
plugins.description: Produce technical architecture and design dossier with ADRs and implementation plans.

plugins.name: sdlc-tech-reviewer
plugins.description: Independent reviewer for Tech deliverables — validates architecture, design, and BA-Tech traceability with conflict escalation to human.

plugins.name: sdlc-test-executor
plugins.description: Execute qualification campaigns and produce structured test reports.

plugins.name: security-reviewer
plugins.description: Review AI artifacts for prompt injection, data exfiltration, and LLM security risks.

plugins.name: spec-orchestrator
plugins.description: Lead structured specification-driven flow for software changes and initiatives.

plugins.name: station-orchestrator
plugins.description: Orchestrate sequential AI station execution within the PR validation pipeline (A0–A7).

plugins.name: workflow-orchestrator
plugins.description: Orchestrate station-based workflow pipelines by delegating work to station agents.

plugins.name: adaptive-decision
plugins.description: Make data-driven pivot-or-persevere decisions in BMAD loops by evaluating experiment results against success criteria.

plugins.name: adr-generation
plugins.description: Generate Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) capturing architecturally significant decisions with rationale, alternatives, and consequences.

plugins.name: agent-policy-guard

plugins.name: ai-backbone-pr-checks
plugins.description: Deterministic merge request validation for Copilot assets: frontmatter, workflow YAML quality, and repository-specific test-gap advisories. Runs on GitLab CI.

plugins.name: api-design-principles

plugins.name: api-documentation
plugins.description: API documentation workflow for generating OpenAPI specs, creating developer guides, and maintaining comprehensive API documentation.

plugins.description: Emit structured audit traces for every workflow station execution with correlation IDs, input/output hashes, sensitivity tags, risk scores, model metadata, and redaction status. Supports local JSONL persistence and optional OTLP export.

plugins.description: Query and manage Azure resources via Azure MCP server — validate IaC against live state, check service health, pull infrastructure topology.

plugins.name: brand-accessibility

plugins.description: Adapt applications, websites, portals, design systems, and front-end codebases to a target brand identity while preserving usability and accessibility. Default brand: Sopra Steria. Extensible for other client brands via .apm/knowledge/brand/<client>/

plugins.name: brand-assets

plugins.description: Structured checklist for auditing apps, slide decks, documents, and communication assets against brand and accessibility standards. Default brand: Sopra Steria.

plugins.description: Reusable core rules of a visual identity: logo, colors, typography, icons, imagery, and layout. Foundation for every brand audit or refactor. Default brand: Sopra Steria. Extensible for other client brands via .apm/knowledge/brand/<client>/.

plugins.name: brand-document
plugins.description: Adapt PowerPoint, Word, PDF, proposal, dossier, memo, brochure, and similar assets to a target brand identity. Converts Markdown to branded DOCX and PDF via Pandoc. Default brand: Sopra Steria.

plugins.description: Create a concise branded one-pager in Markdown following Sopra Steria brand guidelines.

plugins.name: soprasteria-brand-audit
plugins.description: Assess an application, document, slide deck, page, mockup, or codebase against Sopra Steria branding and produce a structured compliance report.

plugins.name: soprasteria-brand-refactor
plugins.description: Refactor an application, document, presentation, or template into Sopra Steria brand style with audit, strategy, implementation, and validation.

plugins.name: workflow-bmad
plugins.description: Run Build → Measure → Analyze → Decide feedback loop (4 stations).

plugins.name: workflow-bug-fixing
plugins.description: Run structured bug diagnosis and resolution workflow (7 stations).

plugins.name: workflow-feature
plugins.description: Run end-to-end feature implementation workflow (9 stations).

plugins.name: workflow-maturity-assessment
plugins.description: Run SDLC maturity assessment workflow (6 stations).

plugins.name: workflow-modernization
plugins.description: Run guided modernization workflow (10 stations).

plugins.name: workflow-pr-validation
plugins.description: Run PR validation pipeline — A0-A7 stations (11 stations).

plugins.name: workflow-quality
plugins.description: Run code quality validation workflow (7 stations).

plugins.name: workflow-spec-kit
plugins.description: Run specification-only workflow (8 stations).

plugins.name: file-output
plugins.description: Mandate that document-producing agents write deliverables to disk as actual files, not just chat output.

plugins.name: mcp-integration
plugins.description: Shared behavioral rules for all agents when MCP (Model Context Protocol) tools are available or unavailable.

plugins.name: output-metadata
plugins.description: Mandate structured YAML frontmatter metadata on every file produced by agents and workflows under outputs/.

plugins.name: pre-input-validation-ba
plugins.description: - When verifying mandatory input IDs (Phase 0a), match against the BA namespace prefixes above.

plugins.name: pre-input-validation-base
plugins.description: Executed **before any production** by the agent. Verifies that all declared inputs are present, valid, and semantically sufficient. Produces either a GO/WARN decision (continue) or a STOP decision (blocking report instead of deliverable).

plugins.name: pre-input-validation-steer
plugins.description: Steer agents consume BA and Tech deliverables but NEVER modify them. The pre-hook verifies presence and readability only — no status gate needed on upstream deliverables beyond confirming they exist and are parseable.

plugins.name: pre-input-validation-tech
plugins.description: Tech agents consume validated BA deliverables as inputs. The pre-hook must additionally verify:

plugins.name: pre-input-validation-test
plugins.description: Test agents NEVER bypass entry criteria. Unlike BA/Tech/Steer agents that allow WARN mode on some inputs, Test agents operate in **strict BLOCK mode**:

plugins.name: pre-verify-source-manifest
plugins.description: Cryptographic integrity check for `steer-review-report.md` before the Go/No-Go station consumes it. This is an **external, non-LLM** verification step — it runs as a script, not as an agent instruction.

plugins.name: post-adr-completeness
plugins.description: Executed **after** the `tech-adrs` station produces its ADR outputs. Verifies that the mandatory ADR categories are complete, the 3-environment pattern is respected, and DEP integration fields are populated when applicable.

plugins.name: post-confluence-push
plugins.description: Executed **after the quality control hook**, as the last step before returning to the coordinator. Pushes the produced deliverable to Confluence by delegating to the `sdlc-confluence-sync` skill's publish tooling. All front matter updates (`confluenc

plugins.name: post-quality-control
plugins.description: Executed **after the agent has produced its deliverable**, before returning to the coordinator. The agent MUST run this complete checklist and correct any non-conforming items before considering the deliverable complete. All modifications to the deli

plugins.name: post-sprint-fidelity-check
plugins.description: Verify that the implementation plan (IMP-001) is consistent with the sprint planning (PLAN-001). Detect missing items, silent sprint reallocations, and filtered infrastructure enablers.
